rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees that reference component ports, and in implementation scopes for implementation-level bindings.
Preserve AADL architecture by placing AGREE contracts in valid annex locations without introducing package-qualified identifiers not present in the model.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features and declared helpers within the same annex scope to avoid unresolved references.
Do not introduce invented numeric bounds or event-port encodings unless explicitly supported by the AADL model.
Ensure AGREE expressions align with AADL data types and do not assume implicit type conversions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations; implementation annexes are for bindings and assignments.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes or referencing subcomponent ports in type contracts unless represented through valid variables.
Do not encode AADL properties like Compute_Execution_Time or unit tokens (ms, Hz) as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE assume/guarantee syntax and validator-driven repair patterns
Model analyst extraction of component types and implementations
